title: UO Diversity Excellence Scholarship — homelessness + Kareem essays
html:
University of Oregon Diversity Excellence Scholarship — three essays Yahir drafted iteratively. The first ties grants pass's nationally-infamous treatment of homeless people (Martin v. Boise lawsuit, Kirt Morrill's death from a propane heater fire, Kellie Parker's case) to his own family's near-homelessness during the pandemic. The second is the canonical kareem essay from the South Africa trip. The third proposes trio program enrollment as the strategy for not getting lost at a big university.

TL;DR

  • Earliest 2023 record of the grants pass homelessness essay thread; cites Martin v. Boise and the kirt morrill propane heater case verbatim. Used again in 2023 02 28 Ford Family Foundation DRAFT Emilio Gonzalez and 2023 11 07 Redwood Foundation Essays.
  • The kareem story (33-year driver in south africa, Yahir sat up front while the rest of the group ignored him) is the most-recycled essay in Yahir's whole 2023 application set — appears in nearly every scholarship from this year.
  • Confirms Yahir was applying to UO under an "immigration lawyer" career frame, despite already being CS-major-bound at osu.
  • Names trio program / TRiO Talent Search as the support network he relied on in HS and intends to seek in college.
  • Cites the uo 3 plus 3 program as a path he's targeting.

Why it matters in the graph

This is the source-of-record for both the grants pass homelessness essay thread and the kareem essay. When grepping for either, land here first; the other 2023 scholarship docs are derivatives.

grants pass homelessness essay thread · kareem · south africa · grants pass · trio program · ms mac · University of Oregon · college application arc · immigration lawyer aspiration
